Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 248 [ritual for good childbirth][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

x x x x ⸢munus⸣sin-niš-tu₄ la mu-še-šer₃-tu₄ it-ti ri-bi ša₂ dUTUši

(o 1) ... a woman not having a normal childbirth: At the setting of the sun you isolate (her). You shave (her) body and you put (the hair) in a new leather bag on her1 neck. You thread copper, lapis lazuli, male ...-stone, magnetite and ...-stone on red wool. You wrap around 3 wrappings made of red wool.

o 22

ta-pa-ra-as ina SU gal-la-bu-us te-ep-pu-ši-ma ina kušna-aš₂-tu-qa

o 33

eš-še-tu₄ ina GU₂-šu₂ ta-šak-kan na₄URUDU na₄ZA.GIN₃ na₄AŠ zi-ka-ri na₄ša₂-da-nu DAB.BA

o 44

u na₄GIŠ.BItu₂ ina sa-mu-tu₂ ta-šak-kak 3 li-ip-pi ina sig₂ḪE₂.ME.DA ta-lap-pa-ap

o 55

ina ŠU-MIN 15-šu₂ ta-šak-kan u ša₂ ku-ri-tu₂ UDU.NITA₂ a-di UZU-šu₂ 2 SILA₃ ŠE.NUMUN

(o 5) You put (this) in her right hand and you give to her from the shin of a ram together with its meat, (and) 2 litres of seeds in addition. It rests on her head overnight. In the morning before the sun rises you hang it from a wall.

o 66

it-ti-i ta-nam-di-is-su ina re-ši-šu₂ i-ba-a-tu₂ ina še-e-ri la-ma dUTU

o 77

na-pa-a-ḫu ul-tu UGU i-ga-ri tu-ša₂-qal-la-al-šu₂ il-lak-ma ina a-šar pa-ar-su

(o 7) She goes away and in a separate place you put bread, meat and seeds on a blocked road, and she says 5 times 'Authorised people have given to me, unauthorised people have received from me'.

o 88

ina UGU KASKAL-MIN pa-rik-tu₄ NINDA-ḪI.A UZU u ŠE.NUMUN ta-šak-kan-ma na-bu-u₂ it-tan-nu-nu

o 99

la na-bu-u₂ in-da-ḫar-u₂-uʾ-in-ni 5-šu₂ i-qab-bi e-ma-aʾ iq-ta-bu-u₂

(o 9) When she has said (this), she strips off her garment and you bathe her in water. She stands up and puts on her second garment, and she does not look back.

o 1010

TUG₂-su i-šaḫ-ḫaṭ-ma A-MEŠ tu-ra-am-ma-ak-šu₂ i-te-eb-<be>-e-ma

o 1111

TUG₂-su 2u₂ il-lab-ba-aš₂ u le-es-su a-na ku-tal-li-šu₂ ul i-na-an-di

o 1212

a-na ID₂ il-lak-ma a-na ID₂ ur-rad A-MEŠ a-na mu-qal-pi-tu₄ 3-šu₂ ib-bak

(o 12) She goes to a watercourse and goes down into the watercourse. She draws water (heading) downstream 3 times and you recite the (following) incantation over it.

o 1313

u EN₂ ana muḫ-ḫi-šu₂ ta-man-nu EN₂ <ID₂> eš-re-tu₄ šu-šu-ru mu-ka muḫ-ra-an-ni-ma

(o 13) Incantation: 'Watercourse, you are just. Your waters are in order. Receive from me transgression, guilt, sin, deficiency, evil (and) the weakness of my body, and carry (them) off downstream with your water. Let the watercourses fill. Let the marshes spread. Let the water in which you are submerged set free the knot of my evil.

o 1414

ar₂-nu šer₃-tu₂ gil₂-lat ḫi-ṭi-tu₄ lum-nu me₂-neš-tu₄ ša₂ zu-um-ri-ia it-ti A-MEŠ-ka

o 1515

a-na qid-da-tu₄ ta-bal ID₂-MEŠ lim-la-aʾ a-gam-mu li-ṭe-e-pi A ṭa-ab-a-ta

o 1616

⸢li⸣-še-ṣa-aʾ ri-kis lum-ni-ia ID₂ eš-re-tu₂ šu-šu-ru mu-ka šu-ši-ra-an-ni-ma

(o 16) 'Watercourse, you are just. Your waters are in order. Put me in order and let me sing your praises. The incantation is not mine. The incantation is Ea's and Asalluhi's.

o 1717

da₃-li₂-li₂-ka lud-lul TU₆ ul ia-a-tu-un EN₂ dIDIM u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI

o 1818

EN₂ dDA.MU u dGU.LA EN₂ dNIN.GIRIM₃ be-let EN₂ te EN₂

(o 18) 'The incantation is Damu's and Gula's. The incantation is Ningirim's, the lady of the incantation.' Formula of the incantation.

o 1919

3-šu₂ ta-qab-bi ⸢NAGA⸣ ta-nam-di-is-su u₃ EN₂ a-na muḫ-ḫi-šu₂ ŠIDnu

(o 19) You say (this) 3 times. You give her potash and you recite the (following) incantation over it.

o 2020

EN₂ NAGA NAGA d30 i-RI-ka dUTU u₂-rab-bi-ka

(o 20) Incantation: 'Potash, potash, Sin engendered you, Šamaš reared you.

o 2121

dad-da-a ina ur-⸢pe⸣-e-ti A-MEŠ ⸢iš-ta-qi₂-ka I⸣ x x ⸢KA MA⸣ x x x x ⸢ŠU-MIN⸣ A A x

(o 21) 'Adad in the clouds gave you water to drink. ... hands ....

o 2222

ša₂ ka-šap DU₃-šu uš-taḫ-ḫi-it ša₂ kaš-šap-tu₄ DU₃-šu uš-taḫ-ḫi-it

(o 22) 'I have washed off what the enchanter has done. I have washed off what the enchantress has done.

o 2323

ša₂ e-piš DU₃-⸢šu⸣ uš-taḫ-ḫi-it ša₂ e-piš-tu₄ DU₃-šu uš-taḫ-ḫi-it

(o 23) 'I have washed off what the sorceror has done. I have washed off what the sorceress has done.

o 2424

ša₂ muš-te-pi-šu₂ ⸢DU₃⸣-šu uš-taḫ-ḫi-it ka-šap u kaš-šap-tu₄ e-piš u e-piš-tu₄

(o 24) 'I have washed off what the magician has done. Enchanter and enchantress, sorceror and sorceress should not judge. You should (instead) be bitumen and should be stuck to a wall.' Formula of the incantation.

o 2525

⸢la? i-di?⸣-nu lu?-⸢u₂⸣ iṭ-ṭu₂-um-ma lu-u₂ en-de-tu-nu i-gar₃ te EN₂

o 2626

TA ID₂ il-lam-ma ana muḫ-ḫi a-tu-nu BAḪAR₂ il-lak-ma a-tu-nu i-ḫaṣ-ṣi₂-in-ma

(o 26) She comes out from the watercourse and goes up to a potter's kiln and embraces the kiln and speaks as follows:

o 2727

ki-a-am i-qab-bi ⸢a⸣-tu-nu el-let DUMU.MUNUS da-nu₃ ra-bi-tu₄ ša₂ ina lib₃-bi-šu₂ na-an-ḫu-za-at

(o 27) 'Pure kiln, great daughter of Anu, in whose middle a fire is burning. Abdomen, in whose middle warlike Giru has established his dwelling, you are sound and your equipment is sound. .... You fill and you empty, but I am pregnant and I cannot deliver soundly what is in my womb.

o 2828

IZI ḪAŠ₂? ša₂? ina lib₃-bi-šu₂ dGIRA₂ qar-du ir-mu-u₂ šu-bat-su šal-ma-ti-ma

o 2929

u₂-de-e-ka šal-mu KUR x KUR? ta-ma-al-li-ma u ta-ri-qa u a-na-ku er-re-e-ma

o 3030

ša₂ ina lib₃-bi-ia ul u₂-⸢šal⸣-lam šal-ma-nu-ut-ka bi-in-nim-ma la šal-ma-nu-ta-ma

(o 30) 'Give that soundness of yours to me and take away that unsoundness. The equipment ... [...] ... should not come out from your middle, but for me -- may what is in my womb be sound as I am pregnant -- let me see (the child). In the house in which I live may it be pleasing before me.' Formula of the incantation.

o 3131

le-qe₂-e u₂-du-u₂ ⸢GI?⸣ [x] MU TA ŠA₃-ka la il-la-aʾ u ana-ku ša₂ ina lib₃-bi-ia

o 3232

liš-lim-ma ⸢er?⸣-re lu-⸢mu⸣-ur ina E₂ aš₂-bak pa-ni lu-u₂ maḫ-rat te EN₂

o 3333

a-na gišKIRI₆ ur-rad-⸢ma⸣ gišGIŠIMMAR i-ḫaṣ-ṣi₂-in-ma gišGIŠIMMAR ma-ḫi-rat kal ša₂-a-ri mu-uḫ-ra-an-ni-ma

(o 33) She goes down to an orchard and embraces a date palm and (speaks as follows):

(o 33) 'Date palm, recipient of all the winds, receive from me transgression, guilt, sin (and) deficiency, and for me in the house in which I live -- woe, alas, not sleeping, diʾu-disease, anxiety, loss of a child, slave or slave woman, whatever there is -- let me not die in my open country.

o 3434

ar₂-nu šer₃-tu₂ gil₂-lat ḫi-ṭi-ti u a-na-ku ina E₂ aš₂-ba-ak u₈-u₂-a a-a la ṣa-la-la

o 3535

di-ʾu di-lip-tu₄ ṣi-it še-er-ra ARAD u GEME₂ ma-la ba-šu-u₂ ina EDIN-ia a-a-mu-ut

o 3636

la i-te-nu-u₂ i-ta-⸢ri⸣ u ši-i qer-be₂-et-ma u₂-da-at? maḫ-rat

(o 36) 'They should not change places with each other, and she is near and ... she is pleasing.

o 3737

a-šar ⸢ḫar-pu⸣ up-ul tu-šab-šu₂ a-šar up-ul tu-šab-šu₂ ḫar-pu

(o 37) 'You produce late barley where there was early barley. You produce early barley where there was late barley.

o 3838

iṣ-ṣi [nap]-⸢ṣa⸣ zum-bi tu-šar-šu₂ iṣ-ṣi la na-šu-u₂ tu-šar-šu₂ in-bi

(o 38) 'You supply a crushed tree with flies. You supply a fruitless tree with fruit.

o 3939

la ṭu-ub ŠA₃ la ṭu-ub ⸢UZU⸣ u ḫa-mu DINGIR-ia₂ u d15-ia ša₂ ina la e-de-e

(o 39) 'I do not know the (causes of my) discontent (and) discomfort and the rubbish (offensive) to my god and my goddess that unwittingly I saw and trod upon. Receive (this) appeal from me and let me sing your praises.' Formula of the incantation.

o 4040

a-tam-ma-ru u₂-kab-bi-⸢su⸣ ul i-de mu-ḫur mu-ḫur-an-ni-ma da-li-li-ka lud-lul te EN₂

o 4141

EN₂ dUTU at-ta-ma ša₂ ka-⸢liš⸣ kib-rat ZALAG ša₂ e-liš u šap-liš EN-šu₂-nu at-ta-ma

(o 41) Incantation: 'Šamaš, you who fully illuminates the edges (of the world), you are their lord of above and below.

o 4242

ša₂ e-piš u e-piš-tu₂ di-ni₇-šu₂-⸢nu⸣ ta-pa-ar-ra-as ša₂ kaš-šap u kaš-šap-tu₂ EŠ.BAR-šu₂-nu ta-⸢aq-bi⸣

(o 42) 'You settle the cases of the sorceror and sorceress. You pronounce the verdicts of the enchanter and enchantress.

o 4343

ša₂ ḫi-bi-il u ḫi-bil-ti ar₂-nu-⸢šu₂ tu⸣-qat₂-ta₅ UB BAD AK KA LA MU MUNUS IN ⸢BI⸣

(o 43) You bring to an end the transgressions of one of injustice and wrongdoing. ... the woman ....

o 4444

ša₂ en-de-et ar₂-nu ša₂ e-piš u e-piš-tu₂ u₂-kal-lu-šu₂ u šu?-lum ša₂ kaš-šap u ⸢kaš⸣-[šap-tu₂]

(o 44) She is one who is inflicted with transgression, who the sorceror and sorceress control, and the health with which the enchanter and enchantress let her carry (her) burden [...] one who is in the process of giving birth to a child but ... does not rear her child and does not extend her family ... [...], does not gaze upon her family, which has been taken away and [...] above.

o 4545

u₂-ša₂-az-ba-lu-uš bil-tu₄ ša₂ še-er-ri ul-la-du-ma x [...]

o 4646

la u₂-rab-bu-u₂ še-er-šu-ma la u₂-rap-pa-šu₂ kim-is-su x [...]

o 4747

la ip-pal-la-su kim-is-su ⸢ša₂⸣ tab-lat-ma AN.TA [...]

o 4848

⸢at⸣-ta-ma dUTU ša₂ ka-liš kib?-⸢rat ZALAG⸣ ša₂ munussin-[niš-ta ...]

(o 48) You are Šamaš who fully illuminates the edges (of the world), who [...] the woman

o 4949

[...] BU US di-in-šu₂ ša₂ kaš-šap [...]

(o 49) [...] ... her case which the enchanter [...]

Reverse
rr  (1 line missing)
r 1'1'

[...] ŠU ⸢LI?⸣ [...]

(r 1') [...] ... [...]

r 2'2'

[...] ⸢še⸣-er-ri ul-la-⸢du⸣ [...]

(r 2') [...] is in the process of giving birth to a child [...].

r 3'3'

[x x] d⸢IDIM? at⸣-ta-ma ba-nu-u₂ ka-la-mu x [...]

(r 3') [...]: 'Ea, you are the creator of everything, ... [...]

r 4'4'

[x] x x NU at-ta-ma UB BAD AK KA munussin-niš-⸢tu₄⸣ [...]

(r 4') '[...] ... you ... the woman [...]

r 5'5'

ša₂ en-de-et ar₂-nu a-na ap-si-i a-na ma-ḫar KA [x] x [...]

(r 5') 'she who is inflicted with transgression [...] to the Apsu before [...] ... the woman.

r 6'6'

munussin-niš-tu₂ e-pu-uš di-nu₃-šu-ma pu-uṭ-ru ⸢ar₂⸣-nu-šu₂ ⸢gil₂-lat-su⸣ [...]

(r 6') 'Resolve her cases and release her transgressions, her sins, [...] and her burden.

r 7'7'

u bil-lat-su ID₂-MEŠ liš-ša₂-aʾ a-gam-ma li-ṭe-pi A ṭa-ab-a?-[ta]

(r 7') 'Let the watercourses carry (them). Let the marshes spread. Let the water in which you are submerged set free the knot of her evil.

r 8'8'

li-še-ṣa-aʾ ri-kis lu-um-⸢ni⸣-šu₂ ar₂-nu e-piš e-piš-⸢tu₂⸣

(r 8') 'Inflict transgression on the sorceror (and) sorceress, the enchanter (and) the enchantress.

r 9'9'

kaš-šap kaš-šap-tu₂ i-mid-da a-na munussin-niš-tu₂ šu-zu-bu

(r 9') 'In order to save the woman, release (the child) from her body. Let me rear the child.

r 10'10'

pu-uṭ-ru ina zu-mur-ri-šu₂ lu-rab-bi še-er-ri

r 11'11'

lu₄-rap-pi₂-iš kim-mit-su da-li-li-ka lid-lul te EN₂

(r 11') 'Let me extend her family. Let her sing your praises.' Formula of the incantation.

r 12'12'

ana MUNUS la₃ SI.SA₂ SI.SA₂ DU₃.DU₃-BI ana IGI dGU.LA NIG₂.NA šimLI GARan

(r 12') Ritual for a woman not having a normal childbirth to have a normal childbirth: You put a censer (full) of juniper in front of Gula.

r 13'13'

mi-iḫ-ḫa BALqi₂-ma kam DU₁₁.⸢GA⸣

(r 13') You libate mihhu-beer and she speaks as follows:

r 14'14'

dNIN.KAR.RA.AK.A GAŠAN ⸢šur⸣-bu-tu AMA reme-ni-⸢ka⸣

(r 14') 'Ninkarak, formidable lady, your merciful mother, may a ewe of Sumuqan and Dumuzi receive from me my pregnancy and may it give to me its pregnancy. May it receive from me my condition of having a difficult childbirth and may it give to me its condition of having a normal birth.

r 15'15'

im-mer-tu₂ ša₂ dŠAKKAN₂ u ⸢d⸣DUMU.ZI me₂-ra-a-a lim-ḫu-ra-an-ni-ma

r 16'16'

me₂-ra-šu₂ lid-di-na ⸢la⸣ mu-še-še₂₀-ru-ti lim-ḫu-ra-an-ni-ma

r 17'17'

mu-še-še₂₀-ru-sa lid-di-na

r 18'18'

3-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma ina ⸢še-ri₃⸣ ana IGI dUTU ina UGU SIG₄-ḪI.A ab-ra MU₂aḫ

(r 18') She says (this) 3 times and in the morning in front of Šamaš she lights a pile of brushwood on top of bricks.

r 19'19'

giššimLI DUBaq ⸢U₈⸣ PEŠ₄ mu-šal-lim-ti

(r 19') She strews juniper. Let her shout in front of the allocation 'Pregnant ewe that delivers safely', and they lift 2 ..., and the pregnant woman speaks as follows into the ears of the pregnant ewe:

r 20'20'

ana IGI nis-ḫi lis-si-ma 2 x x-MEŠ i-na-aš₂-šu u₃ munusPEŠ₄ ana ŠA₃ GEŠTU-MIN U₈ PEŠ₄ kam DU₁₁.GA

r 21'21'

im-mer-tu₂ ša₂ dŠAKKAN₂ [x] ⸢d⸣DUMU.ZI me₂-ra-a-a tab-li-ma mi-ḫir-ki bi-la

(r 21') 'Ewe of Sumuqan [...] Dumuzi, take away my pregnancy and bring me your equivalent.

r 22'22'

la mu-še-še₂₀-ru-⸢ti tab⸣-li-ma mu-še-še₂₀-ru-ut-ka bi-la

(r 22') 'Take away my condition of having a difficult childbirth and bring me your condition of having a normal birth.'

r 23'23'

ana ŠA₃ GEŠTU-MIN-MEŠ ki-lal-⸢le⸣-e 3-TA.AM₃ ŠIDnu ki-ma ŠIDu₂ ina šap-la-an U₈ uṣ-ṣi

(r 23') She recites (this) 3 times each into both ears. When she has recited (it) she goes away from below the ewe.

r 24'24'

x 7i E₃ ša₂ ana x IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ NIGIN-ma UḪ₂-su ana KA U₈ ŠUB-ma ana EDIN E₃-ma TAG₄-šu₂

(r 24') She brings out seven .... She turns her face to the one which is for ... and pours her saliva into the mouth of the ewe, and brings (the animal) out into the open country and leaves it (there).

r 25'25'

ana ⸢MUNUS⸣ [x SI].⸢SA₂ SI⸣.[SA₂ ...] x x x x x x-MEŠ TU? ŠU? UM? LU ⸢MU⸣

(r 25') For a woman [...] having a normal childbirth to have a normal childbirth [...] ...

r 26'26'

[...] x x x ⸢2⸣-TA.AM₃ NINDA-MEŠ ina SILA.LIM₂.MA GARan u₃ ina bi-rit SILA.LIM₂.MA

(r 26') [...] ... 2 times each she puts bread at the crossroads, and in the central space at the crossroads she puts 14 ..., and she says this wording:

r 27'27'

zi₃x x x 14 GAR-ma na-aq-bi-ta an-ni-ta DU₁₁.GA

r 28'28'

na-šu-ma am-ta-ḫar na-ša₂-ku-ma lim-ḫu-ru-in-ni 3-šu₂ EN₂ an-ni-tu₂ DU₁₁.GA

(r 28') 'I have received what they bring. Let them receive what I bring.' She says this incantation 3 times and she puts bread down 3 times. She does not look back.

r 29'29'

u 3-šu₂ NINDA-MEŠ GARan let-su a-na ku-tal-li-šu₂ ul i-nam-di

r 30'30'

ḫa-am-ṣi-ir-ta GAZ-ma A TE A UD TA gišEREN ina ŠU-MIN-šu₂ lu ḪAB šimMUG SAG.DU-su KEŠ₂-ma

(r 30') She kills a female mouse, and you bind ... from cedar on her hands or opopanax (and) ballukku-aromatic on her head, and you wrap (this) with combed wool. She puts (it) at the crossroads. She says this wording:

r 31'31'

ina sig₂GA.RIG₂.AK.A ta-kar-rik ina SILA.LIM₂.MA GARan na-aq-bi-ta an-ni-tu₂ DU₁₁.GA

r 32'32'

na-šu-ma am-ta-ḫar na-ša₂-ku-ma lim-ḫu-ru-in-ni DU₁₁.GA-ma SILA DIB NU DIB

(r 32') 'I have received what they bring. Let them receive what I bring.' She speaks and she does not (again) go along the street which she had gone along.

r 33'33'

an-nam? u an-nam ina la pat ANe DU₃.DU₃uš-ma ina SILA.LIM₂.MA GARan-ma na-aq-bi-tu₂ an-ni-tu₂ DU₁₁.GA

(r 33') She does one thing and the other limitlessly and she puts (them) at the crossroads, and she says this wording:

r 34'34'

na-šu-ma am-ta-ḫar na-ša₂-ku-ma lim-ḫu-ru-in-ni DU₁₁.GA-ma SILA DIB NU DIB

(r 34') 'I have received what they bring. Let them receive what I bring.' She speaks and she does not (again) go along the street which she had gone along.

r 35'35'

EME₃ PEŠ₄ tuš-za-az-ma ŠE.BAR MUNUS ina up-ni-šu₂ IL₂-ma šu-pal EME₃ PEŠ₄ i-ḫal-lu-um-ma

(r 35') You stand ready a pregnant jenny, and the woman holds barley in her cupped hands and slips beneath the pregnant jenny, and she feeds the jenny 3 times and she says this wording to the jenny: 'Let your foetus die and my foetus live.'

r 36'36'

EME₃ 3-šu₂ u₂-ša₂-kal-ma na-aq-bi-ta an-ni-ta a-na EME₃ ⸢DU₁₁⸣.GA ša ŠA₃-ki li-mut-ma

r 37'37'

ša₂!(DIŠ) ŠA₃-ia₂ lib-luṭ 3-šu₂ ina šu-pal EME₃ i-ḫal-lu-up!(BI) u₃ 3-šu₂ ŠE.BAR EME₃ u₂-ša₂-aš₂-ša₂

(r 37') She slips beneath the jenny 3 times and makes the jenny pick up barley 3 times.

r 38'38'

SILA₃? 1/2 ŠE.MUŠ₅ ina SILA.LIM₂.MA GARan-ma ina ap-ti tal-lal-ma munusPEŠ₄ x U₃.TU lu u₂-lap-pat-ma

(r 38') You put a sila and a 1/2 of šigūšu-barley at the crossroads and you hang (it) at a window, and the ... pregnant woman should grasp the birth, and on the day of her labour pains the woman grinds the child and kneads it in the water of her labour pains and makes an effigy of a man.

r 39'39'

ina u₄-um ḫi-li-ša₂ MUNUS TUR i-ṭe₄-en-ma ina A ḫi-li-ša₂ i-la-aš₂-šu-uš-ma NU NITA DU₃uš

r 40'40'

x x U? NU MUNUS DU₃uš EN mi-šil GE₆ te-ru-ba ina mi-šil GE₆ ana SILA ŠUBdi lu ana KASKAL ŠUBuš-šu₂

(r 40') .... She makes an effigy of a woman. You come in during the middle of the night. In the middle of the night you throw (it) into the street or they let it fall into the road.

r 41'41'

[...] x x x x x x x x x-ma ana E₂-šu₂ KU₄ub 3 ne-pe-ša₂-ni GABA.RI? IRI?  ; KAR? ⸢EN?⸣ KUR.KUR

(r 41') [...] ... and she enters her house. 3 particular rituals. Copy of the city (and) of the quay. Lord of the lands.

(colophon)
r 42'42'

⸢GIM⸣ LIBIR.RA-ša ša-⸢ṭi₅⸣-[ir-ma ... md]x—KA.KEŠ₂ bu₁₂-kur₂ mdša-maš₂—SUMna

(r 42') Written according to its original and [...] Anu-ikṣur, son of Šamaš-iddina, descendant of Šangi-Ninurta, [...] Anšar and Kišar he should not take (the tablet) away.

r 43'43'

⸢ŠA₃⸣.AB₂.BA₄.BA₃.ALLA lu₂SANGA—⸢d⸣[x ... AN].⸢ŠAR₂?⸣ u dKI.ŠAR₂ la₃ i₁₁-ta?-ab₂-ba₃-alla

r 44'44'

[...] ⸢me-re⸣-eš-ti la₃ u₂-⸢ša?⸣-am-6

(r 44') [...] intent he should not let (it) be forgotten.

1 -šu "him " and "hiš " are often used for -ši and -ša "her " throughout this text.
